stories.title: Independent autopsy of Nolan Wells finds ‘undetermined’ cause of death
stories.summary: An independent autopsy of 18-year-old Nolan Xavier Wells found his cause of death "undetermined," with no fractures or deep tissue injuries reported. The preliminary findings, announced Wednesday at the NAACP National Convention in Chicago, noted a "red discoloration" on the back of his skull as inconclusive. Wells’ body was found floating off a Mississippi island two days after he traveled there with friends on July 4. The forensic pathologist cited decomposition challenges in the report.

stories.title: Cajun Navy accuses Ben Crump of misrepresenting its words about Nolan Wells
stories.summary: The United Cajun Navy disputed attorney Ben Crump’s claim that Nolan Wells was found fully clothed during a July 22, 2026 news conference about the teen’s independent autopsy. The group stated Wells was wearing only swim trunks when recovered, calling the assertion a misrepresentation. Crump had cited a member’s account of Wells being face-down. The Cajun Navy emphasized it only shares verified facts. Wells’ mother, Christine Wonsley, attended the event.

stories.title: Arrowhead hits ‘best-case scenario’ with latest results for fat-lowering drug
stories.summary: Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals' plozasiran, sold as Redemplo, cut median triglyceride levels by 79% and 81% in two late-stage trials of adults with severe hypertriglyceridemia, compared to 27% with placebo. The drug, a synthetic RNA therapy given every three months, also significantly reduced cases of acute pancreatitis linked to high triglycerides. Results released Wednesday support Arrowhead’s push to expand the drug’s approved use beyond its current rare disease indication. The company plans further regulatory submissions based on these findings.

stories.title: Pittsburgh Pirates @ New York Yankees: Live game updates, stats, play-by-play
stories.summary: The New York Yankees and Pittsburgh Pirates will play a doubleheader Wednesday at Yankee Stadium after rain postponed Tuesday’s game. The first pitch is set for 1:05 p.m. ET, featuring Gerrit Cole against Mitch Keller. The Yankees lead 56-44, while the Pirates sit at 52-49. Fans with Tuesday’s tickets can use them for the 7:05 p.m. makeup game or exchange them for another contest.

stories.title: Napoli, la prima formazione di Allegri per l'amichevole contro l'Arezzo
stories.summary: Napoli kicks off its pre-season with a friendly against Arezzo today in Dimaro. Massimiliano Allegri’s first lineup features Meret in goal, with Di Lorenzo, Rrahmani, Rafa Marin and Gutierrez in defense. The match marks the start of Napoli’s summer campaign as Serie A sides ramp up preparations.

stories.title: DOJ investigating Rep. Cory Mills: Sources
stories.summary: The Justice Department is investigating Florida Rep. Cory Mills, R-Fla., over his finances, including alleged overseas business dealings. The probe, based in the Middle District of Florida, follows a House Ethics Committee investigation launched in November 2025 into campaign finance violations, sexual misconduct and dating violence allegations. Mills, who serves on the House Armed Services and Intelligence committees, denies wrongdoing. The DOJ has not filed charges.
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: Is Instagram down right now? Users reporting issues with Instagram
stories.summary: Instagram and Facebook users worldwide reported widespread outages on July 20, 2026, with over 3,300 complaints logged for Instagram alone. Core functions like Reels, posting, and feed updates failed, while some users saw only old content or blank screens. The issue affected major U.S. cities and stemmed from a shared Meta system disruption. Meta has not yet confirmed the cause or restoration timeline.

stories.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
stories.summary: Apple is testing a new MacBook Neo with more RAM, likely 12GB instead of the current 8GB, powered by an A19 Pro processor. The updated model may also feature new colors and a potential price increase due to component shortages and Apple’s recent device price hikes. Analysts expect the refreshed Neo to arrive alongside new iMacs with OLED screens. Long-rumored updates like the M6 MacBook Pro and OLED touchscreen MacBooks remain in development.

stories.title: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
stories.summary: Samsung’s new smart glasses, developed with Google and eyewear brands Gentle Monster and Warby Parker, feature a 9-hour battery life and will launch this fall. Four designs were revealed—two boxy black frames and two rounded red-brown pairs—with more styles expected. Lightweight and compact, the glasses include hidden speakers, a single button, and a power switch. Hands-on testing was not permitted during the preview.

stories.title: How the Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Flip 8 phones compare
stories.summary: Samsung’s Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Flip 8 series launched July 2026, hitting shelves August 7th. The Z Fold 8 starts at $1,899 with a 7.6-inch main screen and 5.5-inch cover display, while the Z Fold 8 Ultra tops $2,099 with a 200MP camera and 7.6-inch cover screen. Both models feature AI upgrades and design tweaks, though the Flip 8 sees fewer changes. The lineup targets early adopters, with no backward compatibility for last year’s models.

stories.title: Mahmood and Streeting accused of making ‘cheap jokes’ about prisoner early release scheme
stories.summary: Justice Secretary Alex Norris faced backlash after Labour ministers Wes Streeting and Shabana Mahmood joked about the government’s early prisoner release scheme. In a leaked video, Streeting and Mahmood laughed while calling Norris “the enemy” for overseeing the plan. Both ministers apologized, acknowledging the scheme’s seriousness. The controversy follows criticism over the policy’s impact on public safety.

stories.title: NHS trust investigated for corporate manslaughter after Nottingham attacks
stories.summary: Police are investigating Nottinghamshire Healthcare NHS Foundation Trust for corporate manslaughter following the Nottingham attacks. Valdo Calocane, who had paranoid schizophrenia, killed three people in June 2023 after being sectioned four times between 2020 and 2022. A public inquiry heard warnings about his potential for violence and that he was discharged in September 2022 after missing appointments. The investigation focuses on failures in his mental health care before the fatal stabbings.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City amid a legionnaires’ disease outbreak on Manhattan’s Upper East Side. Health officials report 82 cases and five deaths, with eight still hospitalized. No new cases have been diagnosed in six days, but officials warn figures may change. The outbreak has been linked to 34 cooling towers in 33 buildings where live legionella bacteria were found.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians were sentenced to 11 and eight years in prison in Angola’s capital, Luanda, for terrorism and spying. Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov were convicted over alleged involvement in anti-government protests, disinformation campaigns and election interference ahead of next year’s presidential vote. Authorities linked them to Africa Politology, described as a Russian political network tied to the Wagner group. Both denied the charges and had sought extradition to Russia.

stories.title: Ex-Southern Water boss among four charged over alleged plan to manipulate water tests
stories.summary: Four former Southern Water executives, including ex-CEO Matthew Wright, have been charged with conspiring to defraud regulators between 2012 and 2017. The alleged scheme involved manipulating water tests to avoid £45m in penalties by removing wastewater to falsify results. Wright denies all charges and claims full cooperation, while the other three did not appear in court. Southern Water states the incidents are historic and unrelated to its current operations.

stories.title: Kenneth Branagh to Star in Paramount+ Drama ‘Laird’ Set in Scottish Highlands
stories.summary: Paramount+ has greenlit the drama series Laird, with Kenneth Branagh starring as the lead. Created by Jez Butterworth and John-Henry Butterworth, the show is set in the Scottish Highlands and follows an epic family saga. Production is expected to begin later this year.

stories.title: McInnes banned for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes faces a four-game touchline ban, with one suspended, for calling the late penalty awarded to Celtic in May's 13-0-3-2 win at Motherwell "disgusting." The spot-kick, converted by Kelechi Iheanacho, kept Celtic one point behind Hearts, who clinched the title days later. McInnes, who made the remarks after Hearts' final-day victory, will miss three league games and a League Cup tie. He returns for Rangers' opening Premiership match away to Dundee United on July 31.

stories.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane that shaped aviation safety found 74 years on
stories.summary: The wreckage of a Pan American Airways plane that crashed in 1952 has been found 74 years later, 2,000 feet below the Atlantic off Puerto Rico. The Clipper Endeavor went down after engine failure, killing 52 of the 69 passengers and crew. The disaster led to mandatory airline safety briefings worldwide. Explorers used a sonar drone to locate the Douglas DC-4 aircraft last month.

stories.title: Mamdani walks back pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has walked back his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of legal authority. Mamdani now urges US officials to enforce the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant, though the US is not an ICC member. Netanyahu faces allegations of war crimes, with the ICC warrant issued in response to the conflict in Gaza. The mayor had previously discussed coordinating with law enforcement to enforce the warrant.

stories.title: Former Lloyd's of London boss's relationship breached rules, firm says
stories.summary: Lloyd’s of London says former CEO John Neal breached compliance rules by failing to disclose his close relationship with corporate affairs director Rebekah Clement, which could have been seen as a conflict of interest. The firm found no evidence of a romantic relationship but deemed the lack of disclosure a rule violation. Clement’s lawyer disputes the findings, calling the investigation’s process unfair and plans legal action. Neal stated all parties can now move on.

stories.title: Glen Powell, Richard Linklater Teaming Up for Lou Gehrig Biopic, Lorne Michaels Producing
stories.summary: Glen Powell and Richard Linklater will co-star in an upcoming Lou Gehrig biopic for Universal Pictures. The film marks their third collaboration after Hit Man and Everybody Wants Some. Gehrig, a Hall of Fame baseball legend, holds the MLB record with 2,130 consecutive games played. Lorne Michaels is attached to produce. Release details are pending.

stories.title: McInnes given four-match ban for 'disgusting' Celtic penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been handed a four-match touchline ban, with one suspended, for calling Celtic’s late penalty against Motherwell "disgusting." The decision stems from a controversial handball spot-kick awarded in stoppage time on May 13, which Celtic converted to win 3-2. McInnes, who was Hearts manager at the time, will miss Rangers’ next three league and cup games, including a Premiership opener at Dundee United on July 31. The ban does not apply to Europa League qualifiers.

stories.title: Who is Mykhailo Drapatyi, Ukraine's new commander-in-chief?
stories.summary: Ukraine’s new commander-in-chief is Maj Gen Mykhailo Drapatyi, 43, who replaced Oleksandr Syrskyi on an unspecified date. Drapatyi, a veteran with two decades of service including a 2014 Mariupol operation, is praised for battlefield leadership and humane command style. His appointment follows public pressure and military backing. Next, he will oversee Ukraine’s ongoing defense against Russia’s invasion.

stories.title: French police formally investigate woman over 2025 killing of Briton
stories.summary: French police have formally charged a 70-year-old woman with the April 2025 killing of British-South African mother Karen Carter, 65, in Trémolat, Dordogne. The victim was found with stab wounds near her car; her husband learned of her death via a Facebook post before police confirmation. Investigators initially detained the suspect in 2025 before releasing her, but new evidence led to formal charges this week. The case remains under judicial review with no further details released.

stories.title: Victoria Monét Announces New Album ‘Frequency of Love,’ Releases New Single ‘Reach Out’
stories.summary: Victoria Monét will release her second album, Frequency of Love, on October 2 via Lovett Music/RCA Records. The R&B singer also debuted the new single Reach Out, produced by Kaytranada and D’Mile, with a video directed by Dave Meyers. The project marks her follow-up to her 2020 debut. A tour is expected to follow the album’s release.

stories.title: Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger launch bid for 2032 Afcon
stories.summary: Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger jointly submitted a bid to host the 2032 Africa Cup of Nations. The three military-led nations aim to present a unified proposal focused on regional integration and sustainable football development. Their bid follows Caf’s invitation for host applications for 2028, 2032 and 2036 editions. Despite ongoing security challenges from armed groups, the countries seek to advance their vision for African football.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein was found dead in his Paris home on Monday. Daniel Siad, 69, was discovered in Colombes, prompting an investigation into his death. Siad’s name appeared thousands of times in released Epstein files, though he denied knowing Epstein’s crimes. French prosecutors opened a case and plan a post-mortem examination.

stories.title: Susumu Tonegawa Dies at 86; Nobel Laureate Showed How Antibodies Fight Infection
stories.summary: The first Japanese scientist to win the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, he went on to discover how memories are stored, advancing understanding of how the brain works.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

stories.title: South Carolina and Nevada Fight to Kick Off Democrats’ 2028 Presidential Calendar
stories.summary: The calendar order could be hugely important to the 2028 presidential primary race. Democratic insiders will try to sketch out a schedule this week, and rival states are sniping at one another.

stories.title: Trump Administration to Announce Deal That Could Let Saudis Enrich Nuclear Fuel
stories.summary: U.S. lawmakers and Israeli officials have expressed opposition to such a plan, to be announced Wednesday, fearing that the kingdom could use a civilian nuclear project to develop nuclear weapons.

stories.title: Rubio and Chinese Diplomat Make Plans for Trump and Xi’s Washington Summit
stories.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spent much of a meeting with China’s foreign minister discussing the summit, but did not bring up President Trump’s claims of election interference by China.

stories.title: Trump Administration Plans to Stick With Tariffs, Trade Representative Says
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
stories.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

offbeat.summary: The New York Yankees and Pittsburgh Pirates will play a doubleheader Wednesday at Yankee Stadium after rain postponed Tuesday’s game. Fans with Tuesday’s tickets can use them for the 7:05 p.m. makeup game or exchange them for another contest.
